# Real-World Scenarios

### Interview: You're asked to explain a caching system design. What happens if you've always relied on AI to design systems?

   A total reliance on AI to design systems would mean that I would struggle badly during the interview. System design questions are not about memorization of answers, so without proper understanding, I would not be able to explain or do anything.

### Production Bug: At 2 AM, you must debug code generated by AI months ago - but AI is offline. Can you fix it?

   In this scenario relying on AI would be dangerous, especially if that same AI is offline. But if I used AI fairly while learning, i would be able to read the code, understand what it mean, what it is trying to do and see where it might be failing.

### New Technology: A new library is released, and AI hasn't been trained on it yet. How do you learn it?

   By going old school, this means I would need to rely on the fundamentals, read offical documentation, experiment with small examples and connect the new library to what I already understand.

### Reflection: Write one paragraph explaining how using AI fairly today prepares you for real-world challenges like these?

   Using AI fairly today prepares me for real-world challenges by helping me build understanding rather that using shortcuts because new technologies require curiosity and fundamentals. So I’m preparing myself to operate independently even when AI is unavailable, outdated or even wrong.
